signals.reasoning: Macro tape is still weak, but the active veto and headline sensitivity make fresh directional exposure unattractive here. Better to keep BTC neutral than force a late short or a reduced-confidence long.
ETH is stabilizing rather than cleanly breaking down, and the board is back under a live veto. No fresh edge is strong enough here to justify overriding the event brake.
SOL still sits in a weak macro regime, but it is already post-flush rather than a clean new short. Under veto, this is watch-only instead of something to chase.
JTO still has squeeze fuel on funding, but the move is already extended and event-sensitive. Best raw long on paper is not the same as an actual long to take under veto.
GOAT remains damaged, but it is already too unstable and too far along to trust as a fresh short. This is weak structure without a clean new entry.

0G retains the strongest squeeze metrics on the sheet, but it is also thin and already too extended to chase while veto is active. Keep it on the watchlist rather than forcing a headline-sensitive long.
STABLE has strong squeeze fuel and real volume, but it already ran hard and remains directly exposed to the live event brake. That makes it a watch-only long candidate, not an active buy.
GRASS is constructive enough to avoid shorting, but not clean enough to buy through a veto regime. The setup is still too mixed to justify fresh exposure.
DASH already flushed hard and is now bouncing inside a damaged range. That leaves it too extended to short and too broken to trust as a long.
DYM is sitting in a damaged post-flush structure without enough recovery quality to trust a squeeze long. Under veto, the right call is to leave it alone.
HEMI still looks weak, but the downside continuation is already well advanced and there is no clean fresh breakdown to press here. Better to avoid a late short under veto.
VVV is no longer a clean fade at this spot and still does not offer a clean, low-risk reversal entry. The event brake pushes this back to neutral.
IMX has squeeze ingredients, but liquidity is still mediocre and the setup is not strong enough to justify buying through a live veto. Watch for a cleaner follow-through instead.
LDO is leaning bearish mechanically, but not with enough urgency to justify a fresh short after the flush. Under veto, weak-but-late setups stay neutral.
DOT has crowded-short fuel, but price is already down materially and the board is under active event risk. That makes it more likely to chop than to offer a clean fresh entry.
AVNT looks more like a prior liquidation flush than a clean continuation setup. Not strong enough to buy, not clean enough to short.
FTT is far too illiquid and wide to trust, especially with event risk elevated. This is exactly the kind of name we should leave alone.
ENA reads more like a short-covering rally than a clean continuation in either direction. That keeps it off the actionable list while veto is live.
BRETT is bouncing, but not with a strong enough structural edge to warrant a fresh long under event risk. The move is too noisy to trust.
BLUR already looks post-flush rather than cleanly bearish, and the squeeze case is still too weak to buy. Neutral is the disciplined call here.
